name: Athena RPC: device metadata (version, public key, SSH keys, GitHub username)
description: Add four parameterless openpilot athenad RPC methods to the server-initiated RPC layer so the backend can query device metadata over the existing WebSocket channel. The four methods are getVersion, getPublicKey, getSshAuthorizedKeys, and getGithubUsername. Reference implementation on the device side: openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py lines 358-538 (../../openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py). These wrappers let the web UI show openpilot version, authorized SSH keys, and the GitHub username configured on the device.
acceptance_criteria: internal/ws/methods.go gains four new server-to-device Call* functions following the existing CallGetNetworkType pattern: CallGetVersion, CallGetPublicKey, CallGetSshAuthorizedKeys, CallGetGithubUsername
CallGetVersion returns a map[string]string with fields: openpilot_version, openpilot_agnos_version, openpilot_git_commit, openpilot_git_branch (matching openpilot getVersion())
CallGetPublicKey, CallGetSshAuthorizedKeys, CallGetGithubUsername each return (string, error)
RegisterDefaultHandlers in internal/ws/methods.go is extended to install device-side stub handlers for all four methods so hub tests can exercise round-trip behavior
internal/ws/methods_test.go gains a subtest for each new Call* function using the same test harness as TestCallUploadFileToUrl / TestCallGetNetworkType
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
